ddls:
CREATE TABLE "mining_data" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data point */,
  "site_id" INTEGER /* ID of the mining site */,
  "time_velocity" REAL /* Time-velocity of coal mining (m/min) */,
  "safety_efficiency" REAL /* Safety efficiency (average follower distance) (width) */,
  "max_empty_roof_distance" REAL /* Maximum empty roof distance (width) */,
  "avg_coal_production" REAL /* Average quality of coal production (t) */,
  "date_time" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the data was recorded */,
  "operator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operator who recorded the data */,
  "equipment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the equipment used for mining */,
  "mine_depth" REAL /* Depth at which the data was collected */,
  "environmental_conditions" TEXT /* Environmental conditions (e.g., temperature, humidity, air quality) */,
  "quality_control" TEXT /* Results of quality control checks */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_mining_data_site_id FOREIGN KEY ("site_id") REFERENCES mining_sites ("site_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_mining_data_operator_id FOREIGN KEY ("operator_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "mining_sites" (
  "site_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each site */,
  "site_name" TEXT /* Name of the site */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the site */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the site */,
  "site_type" TEXT /* Type of mining site (e.g., surface, underground) */,
  "site_status" TEXT /* Current status (e.g., active, closed) */,
  "open_date" TEXT /* Date when the site was opened */,
  "close_date" TEXT /* Date when the site was closed */,
  "site_manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the site manager */,
  "site_capacity" REAL /* Maximum capacity of the site in terms of production */,
  "site_safety_rating" REAL /* Safety rating of the site */,
  "site_environmental_rating" REAL /* Environmental impact rating of the site */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("site_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_mining_sites_site_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("site_manager_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "parameters" (
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each parameter */,
  "parameter_name" TEXT /* Name of the parameter */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the parameter */,
  "parameter_category" TEXT /* Category of the parameter (e.g., safety, production, environmental) */,
  "measurement_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency at which the parameter is measured */,
  "critical_value" REAL /* Critical value for the parameter that triggers alerts */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp for the last update of the parameter details */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("parameter_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "measurements" (
  "measurement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each measurement */,
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parameter being measured */,
  "site_id" INTEGER /* ID of the site where the measurement was taken */,
  "measurement_value" REAL /* Value of the measurement */,
  "measurement_time" TEXT /* Time when the measurement was taken */,
  "operator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operator who took the measurement */,
  "equipment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the equipment used for the measurement */,
  "environmental_conditions" TEXT /* Environmental conditions (e.g., temperature, humidity, air quality) */,
  "quality_control" TEXT /* Results of quality control checks for the measurement */,
  "measurement_notes" TEXT /* Any additional notes or comments about the measurement */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("measurement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_measurements_site_id FOREIGN KEY ("site_id") REFERENCES mining_sites ("site_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_measurements_parameter_id FOREIGN KEY ("parameter_id") REFERENCES parameters ("parameter_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_measurements_operator_id FOREIGN KEY ("operator_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "site_id" INTEGER /* ID of the site for which the report was generated */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date when the report was generated */,
  "report_status" TEXT /* Status of the report (e.g., draft, final) */,
  "generated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who generated the report */,
  "report_notes" TEXT /* Any additional notes or comments about the report */,
  "report_format" TEXT /* Format of the report (e.g., PDF, Excel, HTML) */,
  "report_size" REAL /* Size of the report file */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_generated_by FOREIGN KEY ("generated_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_site_id FOREIGN KEY ("site_id") REFERENCES mining_sites ("site_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., mining engineer, production manager, executive) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Contact phone number of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level (e.g., read, write, admin) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp for the last login */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for security */,
  "password_salt" TEXT /* Salt used in the password hashing */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the system */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Time when the user accessed the system */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "device" TEXT /* Device used for access (e.g., desktop, mobile) */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the user when accessing the system */,
  "session_id" TEXT /* Unique session ID for the access event */,
  "access_notes" TEXT /* Any additional notes or comments about the access event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
